zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Porter Jr於1505年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾米可萊頓時，他的父親William Porter35歲，母親Margaret Eleanor Gifford46歲。他於1524年與Margaret Atwood在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾格盧斯特結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1558年在英國格盧斯特斯爾阿列沃斯享年53歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Porter Jr于1505年出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾米可萊頓时，他的父亲William Porter35岁，母亲Margaret Eleanor Gifford46岁。他于1524年与Margaret Atwood在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾格盧斯特结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于1558年在英国格盧斯特斯爾阿列沃斯享年53岁。

nameMeanings.name: Porter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper of a great house, castle, or monastery, from Middle English and Older Scots porter(e), port(o)ur ‘doorkeeper, gatekeeper’ (Anglo-Norman French port(i)er, portur, Latin portarius). The office often came with accommodation, lands, and other privileges for the bearer, and in some cases was hereditary, especially in the case of a royal castle. The name has been established in Ireland since the 13th century. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates and equivalents in other languages, for example German Pförtner (see Fortner ) and Poertner .

English: occupational name for a man who carried loads for a living, especially one who used his own muscle power rather than a beast of burden or a wheeled vehicle. This sense is from Middle English port(o)ur, porter ‘porter, carrier of burdens’ (Anglo-Norman French portur, porteo(u)r).
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized, of Poorter, status name for a freeman (burgher) of a town, Middle Dutch portere, modern Dutch poorter. Compare De Porter .

variants.name: Porterfield
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name from Middle English porter(e) ‘gatekeeper, doorkeeper’ + feld ‘open country, bounded piece of land’, referring to the hereditary lands owned by a porter of a monastery (see Porter 1), taken as a surname by the descendants of such an official. Black suggests that they would originally have been known as Porter but extended their name when ‘territorial surnames’ became fashionable.

variants.name: Sluiter
meanings: Dutch: occupational name for a porter or gatekeeper, Middle Dutch sluter. Compare English Porter .

variants.name: Port
meanings: English: from Middle English port(e) ‘gateway, entrance’ (Old French porte, from Latin porta), hence a topographic name for someone who lived near the gates of a fortified town or city, or for the gatekeeper. Compare Porter 1.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a harbor or in a market town, from a homonymous Middle English port(e) (Old English port ‘harbor, market town’, from Latin portus ‘harbor, haven’, reinforced in Middle English by Old French port, from the same source).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Port‐en‐Bessin (Calvados), which is recorded as Portu in 1035–36. The placename derives from Latin portus ‘port, harbour, haven’.

variants.name: Fortner
meanings: German: variant (also Förtner) or Americanized form of Pförtner, an occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper in a great house, castle, or monastery, Middle High German phortenaere (from Latin porta ‘door, gate’). Compare English Porter .

variants.name: Portmann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Helmut, Franz, Hansi, Kurt.

North German: occupational name for a gatekeeper, from Middle Low German port(e) ‘gate’ (from Latin porta) + man ‘man’, or a topographic name for someone who lived near the gates of a fortified town. Compare Porter .

variants.name: Portier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Clovis, Leonce, Renauld.
French: occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper of a great house, Old French portier (from Late Latin portarius). Compare English Porter .
History: Alexandre-Bernard Portier from Paris, France, married Catherine Monte in Edgard, LA, in 1741.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Porter Jr was born in 1505, in Mickleton, Gloucestershire, England, his father, William Porter, was 35 and his mother, Margaret Eleanor Gifford, was 46. He married Margaret Atwood in 1524, in Gloucester, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died in 1558, in Ashleworth, Gloucestershire, England, at the age of 53.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Porter Jr est né en 1505, à Mickleton, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, son père, William Porter, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Margaret Eleanor Gifford, avait 46 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Atwood en 1524, à Gloucester, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé en 1558, à Ashleworth, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 53 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William Porter Jr 1505 in Mickleton, Gloucestershire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Porter 35 und seine Mutter Margaret Eleanor Gifford 46. Er hat 1524 in Gloucester, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom Margaret Atwood geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb 1558 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Ashleworth, Gloucestershire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Porter Jr è nato il nel 1505 nel Mickleton, Gloucestershire, England, il suo padre, William Porter, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Eleanor Gifford, aveva 46 anni. Lui sposato Margaret Atwood nel 1524, nel Gloucester, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì nel 1558, nel Ashleworth, Gloucestershire, England, all'età di 53 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Porter Jr(이)가 1505년 영국 그루스스터실르 믹레턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Porter(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Eleanor Gifford(은)는 46세였다. 그는 1524년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 그루스스터에서 Margaret Atwood(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1558년 영국 그루스스터실르 애슐루르스에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Porter Jr nasceu em 1505, em Mickleton, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Porter, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Eleanor Gifford, tinha 46 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Atwood em 1524, em Gloucester, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1558, em Ashleworth, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, com 53 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Porter Jr родился в 1505 г. в местности микклетон, глоусестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени William Porter было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Eleanor Gifford, 46. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Atwood в 1524 г. в местности глоусестир, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер в 1558 г. в местности ашльворт, глоусестиршир, Англия в возрасте 53 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William Porter Jr nació en 1505, en Mickleton, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, William Porter, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Margaret Eleanor Gifford, tenía 46 años. Murió en 1558, en Ashleworth, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 53 años.
